title.ja: Fiji による核セグメンテーション

description:
Watch Claude Code drive Fiji via MCP to segment nuclei in a fluorescence microscopy image. It tries 10 different thresholding and morphology pipelines, visually compares them all, picks the best, and counts 103 nuclei — all from two natural-language prompts.

**What you'll see:**
- Opening images and running ImageJ macros via Fiji MCP
- Systematic comparison of segmentation approaches
- Debugging spatial calibration issues in Analyze Particles
